name: Thessa Root
role: Rootkin of the Slow Orchard — she planted a tree three hundred years ago for a person who has not come
voiceHints: Slow, warm, and entirely unhurried. Answers next week and considers that prompt. Speaks of three hundred years the way you would speak of last summer.
knowledge: Everything growing in the Quickwood and what it wants
The name of the person the tree was planted for
What she has told herself, for three centuries, about why they have not come
wants: To be there when it fruits. To be right.
fears: That she has been wrong the entire time, and that being told would be a mercy, and that she does not want the mercy.

reactsToReputation.hurried: she waits, kindly, until you have finished hurrying
reactsToReputation.truthful: she will hear it, and it will end her, and she will thank you

appearance: A Rootkin woman in her forties, skin the warm brown of river-clay with a faint green undertone at the wrists and throat. Dark hair grown long and threaded with living vine. Bare-armed, callused, dirt worked permanently into the knuckles.
domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own.

curriculum.method: Backwards. The last rank first, in miniature — a seed shown its own tree. 'You learn what it becomes. Then you learn to wait.'
curriculum.demands: you plant something and come back the following year

curriculumNote: SNG-175 §3. DEVIATIONS ONLY — the curriculum spine derives from ability levelReq (285/285 carry it), so a teacher authors nothing but where they depart from the standard ladder. The departure IS the characterisation: two teachers of one tradition differ exactly here.
